steps.title: Identify the angle relationship at the intersection
steps.content: Two straight lines intersect at a point, creating four angles. The angles $w°$ and $z°$ are positioned opposite each other at the intersection point, which makes them vertical angles.

steps.title: Apply the vertical angles theorem
steps.content:
Vertical angles—the opposite angles formed by two intersecting lines—are always congruent. Since $w$ and $z$ are vertical angles:
$$z = w = 136$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
